task: Project setup — constitution review, session tracking
prompts: general question about tracking agent work, timestamps, prompts
structured format preferred

task: Track 2A — Codegen: client scaffolding, schemas, operations, .d.tl declarations
prompts: new agent task, you're going to work on 2A. first tell me the agent workflow so you know what you need to do
go for it
record as completed session and close out
outcome: Implemented Track 2A steps 1+3: client scaffolding and schema generation. LuaSymbolProvider (ShapeVisitor), LuaWriter (import tracking), LuaImportContainer (require() management), DirectedLuaCodegen (generateService/Structure/Error/Union/Enum/IntEnum), TealGenerator (.d.tl files). Generates 4 files per service: client.lua, types.lua, client.d.tl, types.d.tl. Verified with Weather test service.

task: Phase 2B: schema, serde, JSON codec — foundational serde runtime modules
prompts: read schema-serde SEP, discuss Lua mapping
design discussion: ShapeSerializer/ShapeDeserializer vs collapsed codec
interface proposal review
implement schema, serde, json encoder/decoder, json codec with tests
outcome: Implemented schema.lua (type/trait constants, member lookup), serde.lua (codec interface), json/encoder.lua (pure Lua JSON encoder), json/decoder.lua (pure Lua JSON decoder), codec/json.lua (schema-aware JSON codec with base64, timestamp, NaN/Infinity support). 71 tests passing (40 encoder/decoder + 31 codec). Teal .d.tl declarations for all modules.

task: Track A Step 5: Wire generated client to base client.lua runtime (invokeOperation)
prompts: assess project state, list track A status
work on step 5 — wire generated client to runtime
outcome: Updated DirectedLuaCodegen.generateService() to require base_client, copy invokeOperation onto Client prototype, and wrap base_client.new(config) with generated Client metatable. 5 tests passing: invokeOperation available, service_id/signing_name set, full pipeline flow, error propagation, per-call plugin mutation. TealGenerator unchanged (wiring is internal).

task: Phase 2B: http.lua, auth.lua, crypto (SHA-256, HMAC), SigV4 signer
prompts: scope discussion: auth scheme resolution and signing
confirmed http.lua should be broken out from client.d.tl
implement all modules and tests
refactor client.d.tl to import from http/auth
outcome: Implemented http.lua (request/response types, string_reader, read_all), auth.lua (credentials, auth scheme interfaces), crypto/sha256.lua (pure Lua SHA-256 with LuaJIT bit lib), crypto/hmac.lua (HMAC-SHA-256 RFC 2104), signer.lua (full SigV4 signing). Refactored client.d.tl to import from http/auth modules. 22 new tests (10 crypto, 12 signer), 99 total passing. .d.tl declarations for all modules.

task: Phase 2B: endpoint.lua — Smithy endpoint rules engine interpreter + partition data
prompts: new agent task, confirm workflow
work on endpoint resolution
commit and merge
outcome: Implemented Smithy endpoint rules engine interpreter. runtime/endpoint.lua (rules engine with all standard library + AWS library functions, template string resolution), runtime/endpoint/partitions.lua (AWS partition data for 7 partitions), runtime/endpoint.d.tl (Teal declarations). 34 endpoint tests passing, all existing tests unaffected.

task: Phase 2B: error.lua, retry.lua, retry/standard.lua — error types + AWS standard retry
prompts: new agent task: work on retries, confirm workflow
go for it
outcome: Implemented error.lua (3 categories, constructors, retry classification), retry.lua (retryer interface + none()), retry/standard.lua (AWS standard retry with token bucket, exponential jitter backoff). Rewrote client.lua with retry loop (optional, backward compat). 39 new tests (21 error + 18 retry), 172 total passing. Teal .d.tl declarations for all modules. Updated INVOKE_OPERATION.md.

task: Phase 2B: protocol/json.lua — awsJson1.0/1.1 protocol implementation + schema format fix
prompts: write a test script to confirm generated client works
wait, tell me what you found first
use this session to do the awsJson 1.0 protocol implementation
outcome: Fixed schema format mismatch (table-keyed members matching codegen output). Implemented awsJson1.0/1.1 protocol (protocol/json.lua + .d.tl). Updated schema.lua, schema.d.tl, codec/json.lua, test_codec_json.lua for new format. 17 new protocol tests, 150 total tests passing. Two decisions appended.

task: SQS test harness + fix protocol colon-call bug in client.lua
prompts: write a test harness for the generated SQS client to exercise the full pipeline
did we do this in a worktree or inline
keep inline, but append to SESSIONS.json
outcome: Created test/harness_sqs.lua: wires generated SQS client to real awsJson 1.0 protocol, real SigV4 signer, mock HTTP transport with per-stage logging. 6 harness tests (sendMessage, getQueueUrl, listQueues, API error, per-call plugin override, body validation). Found and fixed integration bug: client.lua used dot-call for protocol.serialize/deserialize but real protocol implementations use colon-call methods. Fixed client.lua to use colon-call, updated all test mocks (test_client.lua, test_codegen_wire.lua, test_retry.lua). Full test suite passing.

prompts: what does LuaIntegration look like, need to think about aws-sdk-lua codegen extensions
can we add a writeCustomSource hook
treat as full session, confirm constitution workflow
go for it
outcome: Added writeAdditionalFiles(LuaContext) default method to LuaIntegration. Wired into DirectedLuaCodegen.customizeAfterIntegrations — iterates over all registered integrations and calls the hook after standard codegen completes. Full build passes including integration test project. Decision appended.

task: Phase 3: Convergence — real AWS call with full pipeline
prompts: new agent task, confirm steps
evaluate HTTP client options before deciding
implement curl_ffi and curl_subprocess
implement env credential provider, STS client, convergence test, run it
outcome: Phase 3 convergence achieved. Implemented: http/curl_ffi.lua (libcurl via LuaJIT FFI), http/curl_subprocess.lua (curl subprocess fallback), http/client.lua (resolver — probes backends in priority order), credentials/environment.lua (env var credential provider). Hand-wrote minimal DynamoDB + STS clients matching codegen output pattern. Discovered STS uses awsQuery (not awsJson), pivoted to DynamoDB ListTables for convergence. Real AWS call succeeded: DynamoDB ListTables returned table 'fugazitab'. Full pipeline: DynamoDB client → awsJson 1.0 → SigV4 → env creds → libcurl FFI → real AWS endpoint.

task: End-to-end endpoint resolution: codegen emits rulesets, runtime binds params
prompts: what is the state of endpoint resolution
design discussion: parameter binding, endpoint_provider configurability
implement end-to-end
outcome: Implemented end-to-end endpoint resolution. Runtime: client.lua binds builtIn params (Region, UseFIPS, UseDualStack, Endpoint) from config + context_params from operation input before calling endpoint_provider. Codegen: EndpointRulesetGenerator.java walks endpointRuleSet trait Node tree and emits Lua table literal. DirectedLuaCodegen wires default endpoint_provider (closure over endpoint_rules + endpoint.resolve) in generated client constructor, emits context_params on operations from @contextParam traits. Added smithy-rules-engine dep. New codegen wire test verifies SQS resolves sqs.us-west-2.amazonaws.com from real rules. All 173+ tests pass.

task: Extract ClientProtocol interface into runtime/protocol module
prompts: protocol interface should exist as a formal module, not duck-typed
perform as full session with worktree
outcome: Created runtime/protocol.lua (documentation-only interface module) and runtime/protocol.d.tl (ClientProtocol + Operation records with correct self signatures for colon-call). Updated client.d.tl to import from protocol module instead of defining inline. Updated protocol/awsjson.d.tl to reference protocol.Operation. Also renamed protocol/json -> protocol/awsjson for clarity (pre-session). All runtime tests pass.

task: Phase 4: restJson1 protocol with full HTTP bindings
prompts: new agent task, confirm steps for restJson1
how are we splitting between http bindings and not
what about inner structure bound to @httpPayload
go ahead
outcome: Implemented restJson1 protocol (protocol/restjson.lua + .d.tl). Full HTTP binding support: URI label expansion (greedy + non-greedy, percent-encoding), query string params (@httpQuery, @httpQueryParams), header bindings (@httpHeader, @httpPrefixHeaders), @httpPayload (structure/blob/string), body-only members via sub-schema with json_name, error parsing (same as awsJson), response deserialization with all HTTP binding types including @httpResponseCode. 29 new tests, all passing. No regressions.

task: Phase 4: All remaining protocols — XML codec, restXml, awsQuery, ec2Query, CBOR codec, rpcv2Cbor
prompts: new agent workflow, confirm where we're at and next steps
implement ALL remaining protocols, awsQuery has ec2Query variant, reference smithy docs
outcome: Implemented all remaining protocols. XML codec (codec/xml.lua): serialize/deserialize with xmlAttribute, xmlFlattened, xmlName, xmlNamespace, minimal XML parser, decode_node exposed for protocol use. restXml (protocol/restxml.lua): REST-style with XML body, full HTTP bindings (same as restJson1), error parsing with noErrorWrapping option. awsQuery (protocol/awsquery.lua): form-urlencoded serialization with Action/Version, flattened/non-flattened lists/maps, xmlName support, XML response deserialization with OpResult unwrapping, ec2 mode flag. ec2Query (protocol/ec2query.lua): thin wrapper setting ec2=true for always-flattened lists, capitalized keys, ec2QueryName trait, no Result wrapper, different error format. CBOR codec (codec/cbor.lua): pure Lua CBOR encoder/decoder using LuaJIT bit+FFI, all major types, half/single/double floats, tag 1 timestamps. rpcv2Cbor (protocol/rpcv2cbor.lua): POST to /service/{svc}/operation/{op}, Smithy-Protocol header, CBOR body, __type error identification. Added ec2_query_name and aws_query_error traits to schema. 81 new tests (27 XML + 22 CBOR + 32 protocol), all existing tests pass. Teal .d.tl for all modules.
